summary: All code_refs into LiveKit agents (Python + JS) and both source quotes (el-orchestration golden nugget, vapi predict-and-scrap) verified against ground truth. Config defaults, line numbers, and mechanism descriptions all hold. Only minor adjacencies (cited ranges, vapi-pipeline file actually named vapi-pipeline-part-1.md) — no errors.

claims.claim: turn.py:128 defines PreemptiveGenerationOptions with enabled=True, preemptive_tts=False, max_speech_duration=10.0, max_retries=3
claims.found: class PreemptiveGenerationOptions(TypedDict) at line 128; _PREEMPTIVE_GENERATION_DEFAULTS = {enabled: True, preemptive_tts: False, max_speech_duration: 10.0, max_retries: 3} at lines 149-154
def on_preemptive_generation(self, info...) at 1872; speech_handle = self._generate_reply(... schedule_speech=False ...) at ~1903-1908
time.time() - info.started_speaking_at > preemptive_opts['max_speech_duration'] return (~1887-1888); _generate_reply with schedule_speech=False and _PreemptiveGeneration object set within 1896-1919

claims.claim: max_retries=3 attempts per turn, counter reset at turn end (agent_activity.py:1985,1894)
claims.found: self._preemptive_generation_count += 1 at 1894; self._preemptive_generation_count = 0 at 1985 (turn completion path)

claims.claim: agent_activity.py:2101-2104 logs saved preemptive_lead_time when speculative result kept
claims.found: logger.debug('using preemptive generation', extra={'preemptive_lead_time': time.time() - preemptive.created_at}) at 2100-2103, inside the context-match branch (2089-2099)

claims.claim: preemptive_tts flag also speculates TTS at agent_activity.py:2572-2580
claims.found: if audio_output is not None and preemptive_opts['enabled'] and preemptive_opts['preemptive_tts']: tts_task, tts_gen_data = await _start_tts_inference() at 2574-2580

claims.claim: Speculative result kept only if context still matches (validation/scrap step)
claims.found: if preemptive.info.new_transcript == user_message.text_content and preemptive.chat_ctx.is_equivalent(...) and tools/tool_choice match: schedule_speech; else logger.warning '...context or tools have changed' and preemptive.speech_handle._cancel() (2089-2109)

claims.claim: JS port identical: _preemptiveGeneration, maxSpeechDuration (10000ms), maxRetries at agent_activity.ts:1391-1438
claims.found: preemptiveOpts at 1391; maxSpeechDuration check at 1406; maxRetries at 1411; _preemptiveGenerationCount++ at 1415; _preemptiveGeneration assigned at 1438. Defaults in turn_config/preemptive_generation.ts: maxSpeechDuration: 10_000, maxRetries: 3, enabled: true, preemptiveTts: false

claims.claim: el-orchestration golden nugget quote: reduces perceived LLM latency by predicting when a user has finished speaking; multiple LLM generation requests with same conversation context within a single turn
claims.found: Line 17: 'The orchestrator reduces *perceived* LLM latency by **predicting when a user has finished speaking. In some cases this results in multiple LLM generation requests with the same conversation context within a single turn.**'

Line 5: 'low-latency orchestration engine purpose-built for real-time conversations, **adding less than 100ms of overhead**'. Note attributes this overall-orchestration figure adjacent to the speculation claim; faithful.

claims.claim: Vapi describes the behavior (multiple same-context requests per turn) / predict-and-scrap
claims.found: vapi-pipeline-part-1.md line 33: 'Endpointing model predicts when the user has likely finished... If the model is wrong and the user continues, scrap that LLM request and start a new one with the updated transcript. This "predict and scrap" method'. (Actual file is vapi-pipeline-part-1.md, not vapi-pipeline-1.md per the mapping note.)

claims.claim: preemptive_tts default-off treats TTS speculation as more expensive/risky than LLM
claims.found: Docstring at turn.py:133-137: 'When False (default), only LLM runs preemptively; TTS starts once the turn is confirmed'. Default preemptive_tts: False confirmed; interpretation is sound.
